ElementDescription: The NDAR Global Unique Identifier (GUID) for research subject
Subject ID how it's defined in lab/project
Date on which the interview/genetic test/sampling/imaging/biospecimen was completed. MM/DD/YYYY
Age in months at the time of the interview/test/sampling/imaging.
Notes: Age is rounded to chronological month. If the research participant is 15-days-old at time of interview, the appropriate value would be 0 months. If the participant is 16-days-old, the value would be 1 month.
M = Male; F = Female; O=Other; NR = Not reported

ElementDescription: Each statement below represents where people are in terms of their thinking about using PrEP to prevent HIV. Please check the number that best corresponds to your thinking about PrEP today.
Notes: 1=I will never need PrEP and have no interest in ever starting it; 2=I am not willing to take PrEP and I currently have no plans start it; 3=I am willing to take PrEP but I don't think I'm a good candidate for it and have no plans to start it; 4=I am willing to take PrEP and think I'm a good candidate for it, but I currently have no plans to start it; 5=I definitely plan to start taking PrEP, but I'm not yet ready to take any steps to get started; 6=I definitely plan to start taking PrEP and I've talked to a doctor about getting started, but don't yet have a prescription; 7=I've gotten a prescription for PrEP, but I'm having trouble getting it filled; 8=I am on PrEP and struggle to take it daily, but am not sure I can see my provider for HIV/STI testing every 3 months, as recommended; 9=I am on PrEP and take it nearly every day, but it's difficult to get to my provider for HIV/STI testing every 3 months, as recommended; 10=I have been taking PrEP every day and am committed to HIV/STI testing and provider check-ups every 3 months, as recommended; -10=Missing
